arts.ttl: Exclusive: West Ham and Crystal Palace 'Want to Hold Talks' to Sign McAtee
arts.sum: West Ham United and Crystal Palace are eager to open discussions over a January move for James McAtee

arts.ttl: Why did Sam Morsy refuse to wear Premier League's rainbow armband?
arts.sum: Ipswich issued a statement, supporting Morsy’s right to choose whether to wear the rainbow armband: “ We respect the decision of our captain Sam Morsy, who has chosen not to wear the rainbow captain’s armband, due to his religious beliefs . the designated ‘ Rainbow Laces weekend ’ encouraged players to show support for the cause, with each team captain given a rainbow armband.

arts.ttl: FA Cup third round draw LIVE: Updates as Liverpool, Manchester United and Premier League sides learn fate
arts.sum: A total of 64 sides will found out their opponents for the next round, with all Championship and Premier League sides entering the draw

arts.ttl: How to watch & follow Ipswich v Palace LIVE
arts.sum: Crystal Palace face Ipswich Town in the Premier League for the first time since February 1995 on Tuesday, 3rd December (KO: 19:30 GMT) – here's how you can watch and follow the action LIVE!

arts.ttl: Ipswich reveal Sam Morsy did not wear rainbow armband due to religious beliefs
arts.sum: British-born Egypt midfielder Morsy is a practising Muslim.

arts.ttl: Ipswich explain captain Sam Morsy’s decision not to wear rainbow armband
arts.sum: Morsy was the only captain not to wear the armband during Rainbow Laces weekend, with Ipswich revealing the player’s decision was due to his religious beliefs

arts.ttl: The key quotes from Oliver Glasner’s pre-Ipswich press conference
arts.sum: The Eagles make the trip to Portman Road on Tuesday night, just three days after the last-gasp scenes at Selhurst Park against Newcastle. Here’s all the key quotes from Oliver Glasner’s press conference, including an explanation of that celebration…

arts.ttl: Bromley create more history by reaching FA Cup third round - Crystal Palace loanee nets winner
arts.sum: The Ravens beat Solihull in the National League play-off final last season to reach League Two and Solihull’s social media accounts posted about wanting to take revenge ahead of the tie taking place. Crystal Palace loanee Danny Imray secured their place in the third round when he latched on to a ball from Ben Thompson inside the penalty area and rounded Solihull keeper Laurie Walker to score.

arts.ttl: How Luke McCowan can be Celtic star with a difference as Odsonne Edouard facing loan limbo - Parkhead bulletin
arts.sum: The Hoops' summer signing has made a telling impact while for one former key man it is quite the opposite

arts.ttl: Going to the game?: Ipswich v Palace matchday guide
arts.sum: Crystal Palace travel to Portman Road for just the third time in the Premier League to take on Ipswich Town on Tuesday, 3rd December (KO: 19:30 GMT).
